s3.bucket.cors.corsrule.AllowedHeaders.Docs: Headers that are specified in the `Access-Control-Request-Headers` header. These headers are allowed in a preflight OPTIONS request. In response to any preflight OPTIONS request, Amazon S3 returns any requested headers that are allowed.

s3.bucket.cors.corsrule.AllowedMethods.Docs: An HTTP method that you allow the origin to run.

s3.bucket.cors.corsrule.AllowedOrigins.Docs: One or more origins you want customers to be able to access the bucket from.

s3.bucket.cors.corsrule.ExposedHeaders.Docs: One or more headers in the response that you want customers to be able to access from their applications (for example, from a JavaScript `XMLHttpRequest` object).

s3.bucket.cors.corsrule.Id.Docs: A unique identifier for this rule. The value must be no more than 255 characters.

s3.bucket.cors.corsrule.MaxAge.Docs: The time in seconds that your browser is to cache the preflight response for the specified resource.
